name: Beef Korma
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds beef or lamb stewing meat, cut into 1-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a blender container, combine coriander seed, cumin seed, cardamom seeds, crushed red pepper, and whole cloves. Cover the blender container and grind the spices into a fine powder.
2. Add 1/3 cup water, blanched slivered almonds, garlic cloves, gingerroot, salt, and ground cinnamon. Cover the blender container and blend until the mixture has a pasted consistency.
3. In a 4 quart saucepan or Dutch oven, brown half of the meat on all sides in 2 tablespoons hot oil; remove. Repeat with remaining meat, adding additional oil if needed; remove.
4. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in the saucepan. Add onions and cook and stir over medium-high heat for 8-10 minutes or until onions begin to brown. Reduce heat to medium.
5. Add blended spice mixture and cook and stir for 3-4 minutes more, or until slightly browned.
6. Add meat and 1/2 cup water to the saucepan. Cover and simmer for 1 1/2 to 1 3/4 hours or until meat is tender. Stir occasionally.
7. Stir together whipping cream, yogurt, flour, and garam masala in a separate bowl. Stir this mixture into the Dutch oven; cook and stir until thickened and bubbly. Cook and stir for an additional 1-2 minutes.
8. Transfer to serving bowl and sprinkle with chopped coriander or parsley. Serve with Indian Spiced Rice or hot cooked rice.
